title: Bridge Essay: The Brain in (Willed) Action: A Meta-Analytical Comparison of Imaging Studies on

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**The Brain in (Willed) Action: A Meta-Analytical Comparison of Imaging Studies on Motor Intentionality and Sense of Agency.**. Front Psychol.. PMID: 31031676. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2019.00804. Voluntary actions can be fractionated in different phenomena: from the emergence of intentions and the ensuing motor plans and actions, to the anticipation and monitoring of their outcomes, to the appreciation of their congruency with intentions and to the eventual emergence of asense of agency. It follows that motor intention and the sense of agency should occur at different stages in the normal generation of willed actions.
